title: Policies for information security
control_objective: Information security policy and topic-specific policies shall be defined, approved by management, published, communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties, and reviewed at planned intervals and if significant changes occur.

title: Information security roles and responsibilities
control_objective: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated according to the organization needs

title: Segregation of duties
control_objective: Conflicting duties and conflicting areas of responsibility shall be segregated.

title: Management Responsibilities
control_objective: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures of the organization.

title: Contact with Authorities
control_objective: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with relevant authorities.

title: Contact with special interest groups
control_objective: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations.

title: Threat intelligence
control_objective:
Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analysed to produce threat
intelligence.

title: Information security in project management
control_objective: Information security shall be integrated into project management.

title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
control_objective:
An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be developed and
maintained.

title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
control_objective:
Rules for the acceptable use and procedures for handling information and other associated assets
shall be identified, documented and implemented.

title: Return of Assets
control_objective:
Personnel and other interested parties as appropriate shall return all the organization’s assets in
their possession upon change or termination of their employment, contract or agreement.

title: Classification Of Information
control_objective: Information shall be classified according to the information security needs of the organization based on confidentiality, integrity, availability and relevant interested party requirements.

title: Labelling of Information
control_objective:
An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in
accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organization.

title: Information Transfer
control_objective:
Information transfer rules, procedures, or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer
facilities within the organization and between the organization and other parties.

title: Access Control
control_objective:
Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be
established and implemented based on business and information security requirements.

title: Identity Management
control_objective: The full life cycle of identities shall be managed.

title: Authentication information
control_objective:
Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management
process, including advising personnel on the appropriate handling of authentication information.

title: Access rights
control_objective:
Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned, reviewed, modified
and removed in accordance with the organization’s topic-specific policy on and rules for access control.

title: Information security in supplier relationships
control_objective:
Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security
risks associated with the use of supplier’s products or services.

title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
control_objective: Relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship.

title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
control_objective:
Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security
risks associated with the ICT products and services supply chain.

title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
control_objective: The organization shall regularly monitor, review, evaluate and manage change in supplier information security practices and service delivery.

title: Information security for use of cloud services
control_objective:
Processes for acquisition, use, management and exit from cloud services shall be established in
accordance with the organization’s information security requirements.

title: Information security incident management planning and preparation
control_objective: The organization shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining, establishing and communicating information security incident management processes, roles and responsibilities

title: Assessment and decision on information security events
control_objective: The organization shall assess information security events and decide if they are to be categorized as information security incidents.

title: Response to information security incidents
control_objective: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.

title: Learning from information security incidents
control_objective:
Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen and improve the
information security controls.

title: Collection of evidence
control_objective:
The organization shall establish and implement procedures for the identification, collection,
acquisition and preservation of evidence related to information security events.

title: Information security during disruption
control_objective:
The organization shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during
disruption.

title: ICT readiness for business continuity
control_objective: ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained and tested based on business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements.

title: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
control_objective:
Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements relevant to information security and the
organization’s approach to meet these requirements shall be identified, documented and kept up to date.

title: Intellectual property rights
control_objective: The organization shall implement appropriate procedures to protect intellectual property rights.

title: Protection of records
control_objective: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorized access and unauthorized release.

title: Privacy and protection of PII
control_objective: The organization shall identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and protection of PII according to applicable laws and regulations and contractual requirements.

title: Independent review of information security
control_objective: The organization’s approach to managing information security and its implementation including people, processes and technologies shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals, or when significant changes occur.

title: Compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security
control_objective: Compliance with the organization’s information security policy, topic-specific policies, rules and standards shall be regularly reviewed.

title: Documented operating procedures
control_objective: Operating procedures for information processing facilities shall be documented and made available to personnel who need them.

title: Screening
control_objective: Background verification checks on all candidates to become personnel shall be carried out prior to joining the organization and on an ongoing basis taking into consideration applicable laws, regulations and ethics and be proportional to the business requirements, the classification of the information to be accessed and the perceived risks.

title: Terms and conditions of employment
control_objective:
The employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel’s and the organization’s
responsibilities for information security.

title: Information security awareness, education and training
control_objective: Personnel of the organization and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness, education and training and regular updates of the organization's information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures, as relevant for their job function.

title: Disciplinary process
control_objective: A disciplinary process shall be formalized and communicated to take actions against personnel and other relevant interested parties who have committed an information security policy violation.

title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
control_objective:
Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of
employment shall be defined, enforced and communicated to relevant personnel and other interested parties.

title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
control_objective: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organization’s needs for the protection of information shall be identified, documented, regularly reviewed and signed by personnel and other relevant interested parties.

title: Remote working
control_objective: Security measures shall be implemented when personnel are working remotely to protect information accessed, processed or stored outside the organization’s premises.

title: Information security event reporting
control_objective:
The organization shall provide a mechanism for personnel to report observed or suspected
information security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner.

title: Physical security perimeters
control_objective: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets.

title: Physical entry
control_objective: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points.

title: Securing offices, rooms and facilities
control_objective: Physical security for offices, rooms and facilities shall be designed and implemented.

title: Physical security monitoring
control_objective: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorized physical access.

title: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
control_objective:
Protection against physical and environmental threats, such as natural disasters and other intentional
or unintentional physical threats to infrastructure shall be designed and implemented.

title: Working in secure areas
control_objective: Security measures for working in secure areas shall be designed and implemented.

title: Clear desk and clear screen
control_objective:
Clear desk rules for papers and removable storage media and clear screen rules for information
processing facilities shall be defined and appropriately enforced.

title: Equipment siting and protection
control_objective: Equipment shall be sited securely and protected.

title: Security of assets off-premises
control_objective: Off-site assets shall be protected.

title: Storage media
control_objective:
Storage media shall be managed through their life cycle of acquisition, use, transportation and
disposal in accordance with the organization’s classification scheme and handling requirements.

title: Supporting utilities
control_objective:
Information processing facilities shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused
by failures in supporting utilities.

title: Cabling security
control_objective:
Cables carrying power, data or supporting information services shall be protected from interception,
interference or damage.

title: Equipment maintenance
control_objective:
Equipment shall be maintained correctly to ensure availability, integrity and confidentiality of
information.

title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
control_objective: Items of equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use.

title: User endpoint devices
control_objective: Information stored on, processed by or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected.

title: Privileged access rights
control_objective: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.

title: Information access restriction
control_objective:
Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the
established topic-specific policy on access control.

title: Access to source code
control_objective: Read and write access to source code, development tools and software libraries shall be appropriately managed.

title: Secure authentication
control_objective: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control.

title: Capacity management
control_objective: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements.

title: Protection against malware
control_objective: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness.

title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
control_objective:
Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained, the
organization’s exposure to such vulnerabilities shall be evaluated and appropriate measures shall be taken.

title: Configuration management
control_objective: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored and reviewed.

title: Information deletion
control_objective: Information stored in information systems, devices or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required.

title: Data masking
control_objective: Data masking shall be used in accordance with the organization’s topic-specific policy on access control and other related topic-specific policies, and business requirements, taking applicable legislation into consideration.

title: Data leakage prevention
control_objective: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks and any other devices that process, store or transmit sensitive information.

title: Information backup
control_objective:
Backup copies of information, software and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in
accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup.

title: Redundancy of information processing facilities
control_objective: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.

title: Logging
control_objective: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected and analysed.

title: Monitoring activities
control_objective: Networks, systems and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.

title: Clock synchronization
control_objective:
The clocks of information processing systems used by the organization shall be synchronized to
approved time sources.

title: Use of privileged utility programs
control_objective: The use of utility programs that can be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled.

title:
Installation of software on operational
systems
control_objective:
Procedures and measures shall be implemented to securely manage software installation on
operational systems.

title: Networks security
control_objective: Networks and network devices shall be secured, managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.

title: Security of network services
control_objective: Security mechanisms, service levels and service requirements of network services shall be identified, implemented and monitored.

title: Segregation of networks
control_objective:
Groups of information services, users and information systems shall be segregated in the
organization’s networks.

title: Web filtering
control_objective: Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content.

title: Use of cryptography
control_objective: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented.

title: Secure development life cycle
control_objective: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied.

title: Application security requirements
control_objective: Information security requirements shall be identified, specified and approved when developing or acquiring applications.

title:
Secure system architecture and
engineering principles
control_objective: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established, documented, maintained and applied to any information system development activities.

title: Secure coding
control_objective: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development.

title: Security testing in development and acceptance
control_objective: Security testing processes shall be defined and implemented in the development life cycle.

title: Outsourced development
control_objective:
The organization shall direct, monitor and review the activities related to outsourced system
development.

title: Separation of development, test and production environments
control_objective: Development, testing and production environments shall be separated and secured.

title: Change management
control_objective:
Changes to information processing facilities and information systems shall be subject to change
management procedures.

title: Test information
control_objective: Test information shall be appropriately selected, protected and managed.

title:
Protection of information
systems during audit testing
control_objective:
Audit tests and other assurance activities involving assessment of operational systems shall be
planned and agreed between the tester and appropriate management.

title: Understanding the organisation and its context
control_objective: The organisation shall determine external and internal issues that are relevant to its purpose and that affect its ability to achieve the intended outcome(s) of its information security management system

title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
control_objective:
The organisation shall determine:
a) interested parties that are relevant to the information security management system

title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
control_objective: b) the requirements of these interested parties relevant to information security.

title: Determining the scope of the information security management system
control_objective: The organisation shall determine the boundaries and applicability of the information security management system to establish its scope.

title: Determining the scope of the information security management system
control_objective:
When determining this scope, the organisation shall consider:
a) the external and internal issues referred to in 4.1

title: Determining the scope of the information security management system
control_objective: b) the requirements referred to in 4.2

title: Determining the scope of the information security management system
control_objective: c) interfaces and dependencies between activities performed by the organisation, and those that are performed by other organisations.

title: Information security management system
control_objective: The organisation shall establish, implement, maintain and continually improve an information security management system, in accordance with the requirements of this International Standard.

title: Leadership and commitment
control_objective:
Top management shall demonstrate leadership and commitment with respect to the information security management system by:

a) ensuring the information security policy and the information security objectives are established and are compatible with the strategic direction of the organisation

title: Leadership and commitment
control_objective: b) ensuring the integration of the information security management system requirements into the organisation’s processes;

title: Leadership and commitment
control_objective: c) ensuring that the resources needed for the information security management system are available;

title: Leadership and commitment
control_objective: d) communicating the importance of effective information security management and of conforming to the information security management system requirements;

title: Leadership and commitment
control_objective: e) ensuring that the information security management system achieves its intended outcome(s);

title: Leadership and commitment
control_objective: f ) directing and supporting persons to contribute to the effectiveness of the information security management system;

title: Leadership and commitment
control_objective: g) promoting continual improvement; and

title: Leadership and commitment
control_objective: h) supporting other relevant management roles to demonstrate their leadership as it applies to their areas of responsibility.

title: Policy
control_objective:
Top management shall establish an information security policy that:
a) is appropriate to the purpose of the organisation;

title: Policy
control_objective: b) includes information security objectives or provides the framework for setting information security objectives;

title: Policy
control_objective: c) includes a commitment to satisfy applicable requirements related to information security; and

title: Policy
control_objective: d) includes a commitment to continual improvement of the information security management system.

title: Policy
control_objective:
The information security policy shall:
e) be available as documented information;

title: Policy
control_objective: f ) be communicated within the organisation; and

title: Policy
control_objective: g) be available to interested parties, as appropriate.

title: Organisational roles, responsibilities and authorities
control_objective:
Top management shall ensure that the responsibilities and authorities for roles relevant to information security are assigned and communicated.

Top management shall assign the responsibility and authority for:

a) ensuring that the information security management system conforms to the requirements of this International Standard; and

title: Organisational roles, responsibilities and authorities
control_objective: b) reporting on the performance of the information security management system to top management.

title: General
control_objective:
When planning for the information security management system, the organisation shall consider the issues referred to in 4.1 and the requirements referred to in 4.2 and determine the risks and opportunities that need to be addressed to:

a) ensure the information security management system can achieve its intended outcome(s);

title: General
control_objective: b) prevent, or reduce, undesired effects; and

title: General
control_objective: c) achieve continual improvement.

title: General
control_objective:
The organisation shall plan:
d) actions to address these risks and opportunities; and

title: General
control_objective:
e) how to
 1) integrate and implement these actions into its information security management system processes; and
 2) evaluate the effectiveness of these actions.

title: Information security risk assessment
control_objective: The organisation shall define and apply an information security risk assessment process that:

title: Information security risk assessment
Information security risk assessment
control_objective: b) ensures that repeated information security risk assessments produce consistent, valid and comparable results;

title: Information security risk assessment
control_objective:
c) identifies the information security risks:
 1) apply the information security risk assessment process to identify risks associated with the loss of confidentiality, integrity and availability for information within the scope of the information security management system; and
 2) identify the risk owners

title: Information security risk assessment
Information security risk assessment
control_objective:
e) evaluates the information security risks:
 1) compare the results of risk analysis with the risk criteria established in 6.1.2 a); and
 2) prioritise the analysed risks for risk treatment.

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: The organisation shall define and apply an information security risk treatment process to:

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: a) select appropriate information security risk treatment options, taking account of the risk assessment results;

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: b) determine all controls that are necessary to implement the information security risk treatment option(s) chosen;

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: c) compare the controls determined in 6.1.3 b) above with those in Annex A and verify that no necessary controls have been omitted;

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: d) produce a Statement of Applicability that contains the necessary controls and justification for inclusions, whether they are implemented or not, and the justification for exclusions of controls from Annex A;

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: e) formulate an information security risk treatment plan; and

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: f ) obtain risk owners’ approval of the information security risk treatment plan and acceptance of the residual information security risks.

title: General
control_objective: The organisation shall establish information security objectives at relevant functions and levels.

title: General
control_objective:
The information security objectives shall:
a) be consistent with the information security policy

title: General

title: General
control_objective: c) take into account applicable information security requirements, and risk assessment and risk treatment results;

title: General

title: General

When planning how to achieve its information security objectives, the organisation shall determine;

control_objective: j) how the results will be evaluated.

title: Resources
control_objective: The organisation shall determine and provide the resources needed for the establishment, implementation, maintenance and continual improvement of the information security management system.

title: Competence
control_objective:
The organisation shall:

a) determine the necessary competence of person(s) doing work under its control that affects its information security performance;

title: Competence
control_objective: b) ensure that these persons are competent on the basis of appropriate education, training, or experience;

title: Competence
control_objective: c) where applicable, take actions to acquire the necessary competence, and evaluate the effectiveness of the actions taken; and

title: Competence
control_objective: d) retain appropriate documented information as evidence of competence.

title: Awareness
control_objective:
Persons doing work under the organisation’s control shall be aware of:
a) the information security policy;

title: Awareness
control_objective: b) their contribution to the effectiveness of the information security management system, including the benefits of improved information security performance; and

title: Awareness
control_objective: c) the implications of not conforming with the information security management system requirements.

title: Communication
Documented information General
control_objective:
The organisation’s information security management system shall include:

a) documented information required by this International Standard; and
b) documented information determined by the organisation as being necessary for the effectiveness of the information security management system.

title: Creating and updating
control_objective:
When creating and updating documented information the organisation shall ensure appropriate:

a) identification and description (e.g. a title, date, author, or reference number)
b) format (e.g. language, software version, graphics) and media (e.g. paper, electronic); and 
c) review and approval for suitability and adequacy.

title: Control of documented information
control_objective:
Documented information required by the information security management system and by this International Standard shall be controlled to ensure:

a) it is available and suitable for use, where and when it is needed; and
b) it is adequately protected (e.g. from loss of confidentiality, improper use, or loss of integrity).
 For the control of documented information, the organisation shall address the following activities, as applicable:

c) distribution, access, retrieval and use; 
d) storage and preservation, including the preservation of legibility;
e) control of changes (e.g. version control); and
f ) retention and disposition.

title: Operational planning and control
control_objective: The organisation shall plan, implement and control the processes needed to meet information security requirements, and to implement the actions determined in 6.1. The organisation shall also implement plans to achieve information security objectives determined in 6.2.

title: Operational planning and control
control_objective: The organisation shall keep documented information to the extent necessary to have confidence that the processes have been carried out as planned.

title: Operational planning and control
control_objective: The organisation shall control planned changes and review the consequences of unintended changes, taking action to mitigate any adverse effects, as necessary.

title: Operational planning and control
control_objective: The organisation shall ensure that outsourced processes are determined and controlled.

title: Information security risk assessment
control_objective: The organisation shall perform information security risk assessments at planned intervals or when significant changes are proposed or occur, taking account of the criteria established in 6.1.2 a).

title: Information security risk assessment
control_objective: The organisation shall retain documented information of the results of the information security risk assessments.

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective:
The organisation shall implement the information security risk treatment plan.
The organisation shall retain documented information of the results of the information security risk treatment.

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective:
The organisation shall implement the information security risk treatment plan.
The organisation shall retain documented information of the results of the information security risk treatment.

title: Internal audit
control_objective:
The organisation shall conduct internal audits at planned intervals to provide information on whether the information security management system:

a) conforms to
 1) the organisation’s own requirements for its information security management system; and
 2) the requirements of this International Standard;

title: Internal audit
control_objective: b) is effectively implemented and maintained. The organisation shall:

title: Internal audit
control_objective: c) plan, establish, implement and maintain an audit programme(s), including the frequency, methods, responsibilities, planning requirements and reporting. The audit programme(s) shall take into consideration the importance of the processes concerned and the results of previous audits;

title: Internal audit
control_objective: d) define the audit criteria and scope for each audit;

title: Internal audit
control_objective: e) select auditors and conduct audits that ensure objectivity and the impartiality of the audit process;

title: Internal audit
control_objective: f ) ensure that the results of the audits are reported to relevant management; and

title: Internal audit
control_objective: g) retain documented information as evidence of the audit programme(s) and the audit results.

Continual improvement
control_objective: The organisation shall continually improve the suitability, adequacy and effectiveness of the information security management system.

The organization shall establish, implement, maintain and continually improve an information security
management system, including the processes needed and their interactions, in accordance with the
requirements of this document.

control_objective:
c) identifies the information security risks:
 1) apply the information security risk assessment process to identify risks associated with the loss of confidentiality, integrity and availability for information within the scope of the information security management system; and
 2) identify the risk owners

title: Information security risk assessment
Information security risk assessment
control_objective:
e) evaluates the information security risks:
 1) compare the results of risk analysis with the risk criteria established in 6.1.2 a); and
 2) prioritise the analysed risks for risk treatment.

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: The organisation shall define and apply an information security risk treatment process to:

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: a) select appropriate information security risk treatment options, taking account of the risk assessment results;

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: b) determine all controls that are necessary to implement the information security risk treatment option(s) chosen;

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: c) compare the controls determined in 6.1.3 b) above with those in Annex A and verify that no necessary controls have been omitted;

title: Information security risk treatment
Information security risk treatment
control_objective: e) formulate an information security risk treatment plan; and

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective: f ) obtain risk owners’ approval of the information security risk treatment plan and acceptance of the residual information security risks.

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective:
The organization shall retain documented information about the information security risk treatment
process.

title: General
control_objective: The organisation shall establish information security objectives at relevant functions and levels.

title: General
control_objective:
The information security objectives shall:
a) be consistent with the information security policy

title: General
control_objective: b) be measurable (if practicable);

title: General
control_objective: c) take into account applicable information security requirements, and risk assessment and risk treatment results;

title: General
control_objective: d) be monitored

title: General
control_objective: e) be communicated

title: General
control_objective: f) be updated as appropriate

title: General
control_objective:
g) be available as documented information

The organization shall retain documented information on the information security objectives.

title: General
control_objective:
When planning how to achieve its information security objectives, the organisation shall determine;

h ) what will be done;

title: General
control_objective: i) what resources will be required;

title: General
control_objective: j) who will be responsible;

title: General
control_objective: k) when it will be completed; and

title: General
control_objective: l) how the results will be evaluated.
When the organization determines the need for changes to the information security management system, the changes shall be carried out in a planned manner.

title: Resources
control_objective: The organisation shall determine and provide the resources needed for the establishment, implementation, maintenance and continual improvement of the information security management system.

title: Competence
control_objective:
The organisation shall:

a) determine the necessary competence of person(s) doing work under its control that affects its information security performance;

title: Competence
control_objective: b) ensure that these persons are competent on the basis of appropriate education, training, or experience;

title: Competence
control_objective: c) where applicable, take actions to acquire the necessary competence, and evaluate the effectiveness of the actions taken; and

title: Competence
control_objective: d) retain appropriate documented information as evidence of competence.

title: Awareness
control_objective:
Persons doing work under the organisation’s control shall be aware of:
a) the information security policy;

title: Awareness
control_objective: b) their contribution to the effectiveness of the information security management system, including the benefits of improved information security performance; and

title: Awareness
control_objective: c) the implications of not conforming with the information security management system requirements.

title: Communication
Documented information General
control_objective:
The organisation’s information security management system shall include:

a) documented information required by this document; and
b) documented information determined by the organisation as being necessary for the effectiveness of the information security management system.

title: Creating and updating
control_objective:
When creating and updating documented information the organisation shall ensure appropriate:

a) identification and description (e.g. a title, date, author, or reference number)
b) format (e.g. language, software version, graphics) and media (e.g. paper, electronic); and 
c) review and approval for suitability and adequacy.

title: Control of documented information
control_objective:
Documented information required by the information security management system and by this document shall be controlled to ensure:

a) it is available and suitable for use, where and when it is needed; and
b) it is adequately protected (e.g. from loss of confidentiality, improper use, or loss of integrity).
 For the control of documented information, the organisation shall address the following activities, as applicable:

c) distribution, access, retrieval and use; 
d) storage and preservation, including the preservation of legibility;
e) control of changes (e.g. version control); and
f ) retention and disposition.

Documented information of external origin, determined by the organization to be necessary for the planning and operation of the information security management system, shall be identified as appropriate, and controlled.

title: Operational planning and control
Operational planning and control
control_objective: Documented information shall be available to the extent necessary to have confidence that the processes have been carried out as planned.

title: Operational planning and control
control_objective: The organisation shall control planned changes and review the consequences of unintended changes, taking action to mitigate any adverse effects, as necessary.

title: Operational planning and control
control_objective: The organisation shall ensure that and externally provided processes, products or services that are relevant to the information security management system are controlled.

title: Information security risk assessment
control_objective: The organisation shall perform information security risk assessments at planned intervals or when significant changes are proposed or occur, taking account of the criteria established in 6.1.2 a).

title: Information security risk assessment
control_objective: The organisation shall retain documented information of the results of the information security risk assessments.

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective:
The organisation shall implement the information security risk treatment plan.
The organisation shall retain documented information of the results of the information security risk treatment.

title: Information security risk treatment
control_objective:
The organisation shall implement the information security risk treatment plan.
The organisation shall retain documented information of the results of the information security risk treatment.

title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
control_objective:
The organisation shall determine:

a) what needs to be monitored and measured, including information security processes and controls;

title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
control_objective:
b) the methods for monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation, as applicable, to ensure
valid results. The methods selected should produce comparable and reproducible results to be considered valid;

title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
control_objective: c) when the monitoring and measuring shall be performed;

title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
control_objective: d) who shall monitor and measure;

title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
control_objective: e) when the results from monitoring and measurement shall be analysed and evaluated;

title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
control_objective:
f) who shall analyse and evaluate these results.

Documented information shall be available as evidence of the results.

title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
control_objective: The organisation shall evaluate the information security performance and the effectiveness of the information security management system.

title: Internal Audit General
control_objective:
The organisation shall conduct internal audits at planned intervals to provide information on whether the information security management system:

a) conforms to
 1) the organisation’s own requirements for its information security management system; and
 2) the requirements of this document;

title: Internal Audit General
control_objective: b) is effectively implemented and maintained.

title: Internal audit programme
control_objective:
The organization shall plan, establish, implement and maintain an audit programme(s), including the frequency, methods, responsibilities, planning requirements and reporting.
When establishing the internal audit  programme(s), the organization shall consider the importance of the processes concerned and the results of previous audits.

title: Internal audit programme
control_objective:
The organisation shall 
a) define the audit criteria and scope for each audit;

title: Internal audit programme
control_objective: b) select auditors and conduct audits that ensure objectivity and the impartiality of the audit process;

title: Internal audit programme
control_objective: c ) ensure that the results of the audits are reported to relevant management;

title: Internal audit programme
control_objective: Documented information shall be available as evidence of the implementation of the audit programme(s)and the audit results.

title: General
control_objective: Top management shall review the organization's information security management system at planned intervals to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.

title: Management review inputs
Management review results
control_objective:
The results of the management review shall include decisions related to continual improvement opportunities and any needs for changes to the information security management system.

Documented information shall be available as evidence of the results of management reviews.

title: Continual improvement
control_objective: The organisation shall continually improve the suitability, adequacy and effectiveness of the information security management system.

title: Nonconformity and corrective action
Policies for information security
control_objective: A set of policies for information security should be defined, approved by management, published and communicated to employees and relevant external parties.

title: Review of the policies for information security
control_objective: The policies for information security should be reviewed at planned intervals or if significant changes occur to ensure their continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.

title: Information security roles and responsibilities
control_objective: All information security responsibilities should be defined and allocated.

title: Segregation of duties
control_objective: Conflicting duties and areas of responsibility should be segregated to reduce opportunities for unauthorised or unintentional modification or misuse of the organisation’s assets.

title: Contact with authorities
control_objective: Appropriate contacts with relevant authorities should be maintained

title: Contact with special interest groups
control_objective: Appropriate contacts with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations should be maintained.

title: Information security in project management
control_objective: Information security should be addressed in project management, regardless of the type of the project.

title: Mobile device policy
control_objective: A policy and supporting security measures should be adopted to manage the risks introduced by using mobile devices.

title: Teleworking
control_objective: A policy and supporting security measures should be implemented to protect information accessed, processed or stored at teleworking sites.

title: Screening
control_objective: Background verification checks on all candidates for employment should be carried out in accordance with relevant laws, regulations and ethics and should be proportional to the business requirements, the classification of the information to be accessed and the perceived risks.

title: Terms and conditions of employment
control_objective: The contractual agreements with employees and contractors should state their and the organisation’s responsibilities for information security.

title: Management responsibilities
control_objective: Management should require all employees and contractors to apply information security in accordance with the established policies and procedures of the organisation.

title: Information security awareness, education and training
control_objective: All employees of the organisation and, where relevant, contractors should receive appropriate awareness education and training and regular updates in organisational policies and procedures, as relevant for their job function.

title: Disciplinary process
control_objective: There should be a formal and communicated disciplinary process in place to take action against employees who have committed an information security breach.

title: Termination or change of employment responsibilities
control_objective: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment should be defined, communicated to the employee or contractor and enforced.

title: Inventory of assets
control_objective: Assets associated with information and information processing facilities should be identified and an inventory of these assets should be drawn up and maintained.

title: Ownership of assets
control_objective: Assets maintained in the inventory should be owned.

title: Acceptable use of assets
control_objective: Rules for the acceptable use of information and of assets associated with information and information processing facilities should be identified, documented and implemented.

title: Return of assets
control_objective: All employees and external party users should return all of the organisational assets in their possession upon termination of their employment, contract or agreement.

title: Classification of information
control_objective: Information should be classified in terms of legal requirements, value, criticality and sensitivity to unauthorised disclosure or modification.

title: Labelling of information
control_objective: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling should be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organisation.

title: Handling of assets
control_objective: Procedures for handling assets should be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organisation.

title: Management of removable media
control_objective: Procedures should be implemented for the management of removable media in accordance with the classification scheme adopted by the organisation.

title: Disposal of media
control_objective: Media should be disposed of securely when no longer required, using formal procedures.

title: Physical media transfer
control_objective: Media containing information should be protected against unauthorised access, misuse or corruption during transportation.

title: Access control policy
control_objective: An access control policy should be established, documented and reviewed based on business and information security requirements.

title: Access to networks and network services
control_objective: Users should only be provided with access to the network and network services that they have been specifically authorised to use

title: User registration and de­registration
control_objective: A formal user registration and de­registration process should be implemented to enable assignment of access rights.

title: User access provisioning
control_objective: A formal user access provisioning process should be implemented to assign or revoke access rights for all user types to all systems and services.

title: Management of privileged access rights
control_objective: The allocation and use of privileged access rights should be restricted and controlled.

title: Management of secret authentication information of users
control_objective: The allocation of secret authentication information should be controlled through a formal management process.

title: Review of user access rights
control_objective: Asset owners should review users’ access rights at regular intervals

title: Removal or adjustment of access rights
control_objective: The access rights of all employees and external party users to information and information processing facilities should be removed upon termination of their employment, contract or agreement, or adjusted upon change.

title: Use of secret authentication information
control_objective: Users should be required to follow the organisation’s practices in the use of secret authentication information.

title: Information access restriction
control_objective: Access to information and application system functions should be restricted in accordance with the access control policy.

title: Secure log­on procedures
control_objective: Where required by the access control policy, access to systems and applications should be controlled by a secure log-on procedure.

title: Password management system
control_objective: Password management systems should be interactive and should ensure quality passwords.

title: Use of privileged utility programs
control_objective: The use of utility programs that might be capable of overriding system and application controls should be restricted and tightly controlled.

title: Access control to program source code
control_objective: Access to program source code should be restricted.

title: Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
control_objective: A policy on the use of cryptographic controls for protection of information should be developed and implemented.

title: Key management
control_objective: A policy on the use, protection and lifetime of cryptographic keys should be developed and implemented through their whole lifecycle.

title: Physical security perimeter
control_objective: Security perimeters should be defined and used to protect areas that contain either sensitive or critical information and information processing facilities.

title: Physical entry controls
control_objective: Secure areas should be protected by appropriate entry controls to ensure that only authorised personnel are allowed access.

title: Securing offices, rooms and facilities
control_objective: Physical security for offices, rooms and facilities should be designed and applied.

title: Protecting against external and environmental threats
control_objective: Physical protection against natural disasters, malicious attack or accidents should be designed and applied.

title: Working in secure areas
control_objective: Procedures for working in secure areas should be designed and applied.

title: Delivery and loading areas
control_objective: Access points such as delivery and loading areas and other points where unauthorised persons could enter the premises should be controlled and, if possible, isolated from information processing facilities to avoid unauthorised access.

title: Equipment siting and protection
control_objective: Equipment should be sited and protected to reduce the risks from environmental threats and hazards, and opportunities for unauthorised access.

title: Supporting utilities
control_objective: Equipment should be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities.

title: Cabling security
control_objective: Power and telecommunications cabling carrying data or supporting information services should be protected from interception, interference or damage.

title: Equipment maintenance
control_objective: Equipment should be correctly maintained to ensure its continued availability and integrity.

title: Removal of assets
control_objective: Equipment, information or software should not be taken off­site without prior authorisation.

title: Security of equipment and assets off premises
control_objective: Security should be applied to off-site assets taking into account the different risks of working outside the organisation’s premises.

title: Secure disposal or reuse of equipment
control_objective: All items of equipment containing storage media should be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use.

title: Unattended user equipment
control_objective: Users should ensure that unattended equipment has appropriate protection.

title: Clear desk and clear screen policy
control_objective: A clear desk policy for papers and removable storage media and a clear screen policy for information processing facilities should be adopted.

title: Documented operating procedures
control_objective: Operating procedures should be documented and made available to all users who need them.

title: Change management
control_objective: Changes to the organisation, business processes, information processing facilities and systems that affect information security should be controlled.

title: Capacity management
control_objective: The use of resources should be monitored, tuned and projections made of future capacity requirements to ensure the required system performance.

title: Separation of development, testing and operational environments
control_objective: Development, testing, and operational environments should be separated to reduce the risks of unauthorised access or changes to the operational environment.

title: Controls against malware
control_objective: Detection, prevention and recovery controls to protect against malware should be implemented, combined with appropriate user awareness.

title: Information backup
control_objective: Backup copies of information, software and system images should be taken and tested regularly in accordance with an agreed backup policy.

title: Event logging
control_objective: Event logs recording user activities, exceptions, faults and information security events should be produced, kept and regularly reviewed.

title: Protection of log information
control_objective: Logging facilities and log information should be protected against tampering and unauthorised access.

title: Administrator and operator logs
control_objective: System administrator and system operator activities should be logged and the logs protected and regularly reviewed.

title: Clock synchronisation
control_objective: The clocks of all relevant information processing systems within an organisation or security domain should be synchronised to a single reference time source.

title: Installation of software on operational systems
control_objective: Procedures should be implemented to control the installation of software on operational systems.

title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
control_objective:
Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems being used should be obtained in a timely fashion, the organization's exposure to such vulnerabilities evaluated and appropriate measures
taken to address the associated risk.

title: Restrictions on software installation
control_objective: Rules governing the installation of software by users should be established and implemented.

title: Information systems audit controls
control_objective: Audit requirements and activities involving verification of operational systems should be carefully planned and agreed to minimise disruptions to business processes.

title: Network controls
control_objective: Networks should be managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.

title: Security of network services
control_objective: Security mechanisms, service levels and management requirements of all network services should be identified and included in network services agreements, whether these services are provided in-house or outsourced.

title: Segregation in networks
control_objective: Groups of information services, users and information systems should be segregated on networks.

title: Information transfer policies and procedures
control_objective: Formal transfer policies, procedures and controls should be in place to protect the transfer of information through the use of all types of communication facilities.

title: Agreements on information transfer
control_objective:
Agreements should address the secure transfer of business information between the organization and
external parties.

title: Electronic messaging
control_objective: Information involved in electronic messaging should be appropriately protected.

title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
control_objective: Requirements for confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organisation’s needs for the protection of information should be identified, regularly reviewed and documented.

title: Information security requirements analysis and specification
control_objective: The information security related requirements should be included in the requirements for new information systems or enhancements to existing information systems.

title: Securing application services on public networks
control_objective: Information involved in application services passing over public networks should be protected from fraudulent activity, contract dispute and unauthorised disclosure and modification.

title: Protecting application services transactions
control_objective: Information involved in application service transactions should be protected to prevent incomplete transmission, misrouting, unauthorised message alteration, unauthorised disclosure, unauthorised message duplication or replay.

title: Secure development policy
control_objective: Rules for the development of software and systems should be established and applied to developments within the organisation.

title: System change control procedures
control_objective: Changes to systems within the development lifecycle should be controlled by the use of formal change control procedures.

title: Technical review of applications after operating platform changes
control_objective: When operating platforms are changed, business critical applications should be reviewed and tested to ensure there is no adverse impact on organisational operations or security.

title: Restrictions on changes to software packages
control_objective: Modifications to software packages should be discouraged, limited to necessary changes and all changes should be strictly controlled.

title: Secure system engineering principles
control_objective: Principles for engineering secure systems should be established, documented, maintained and applied to any information system implementation efforts.

title: Secure development environment
control_objective: Organizations should establish and appropriately protect secure development environments for system development and integration efforts that cover the entire system development lifecycle.

title: Outsourced development
control_objective: The organisation should supervise and monitor the activity of outsourced system development.

title: System security testing
control_objective: Testing of security functionality should be carried out during development.

title: System acceptance testing
control_objective: Acceptance testing programs and related criteria should be established for new information systems, upgrades and new versions.

title: Protection of test data
control_objective: Test data should be selected carefully, protected and controlled.

title: Information security policy for supplier relationships
control_objective: Information security requirements for mitigating the risks associated with supplier’s access to the organization’s assets should be agreed with the supplier and documented.

title: Addressing security within supplier agreements
control_objective:
All relevant information security requirements should be established and agreed with each supplier that may access, process, store, communicate, or provide IT infrastructure components for, the
organization’s information.

title: Information and communication technology supply chain
control_objective: Agreements with suppliers should include requirements to address the information security risks associated with information and communications technology services and product supply chain.

title: Monitoring and review of supplier services
control_objective: Organisations should regularly monitor, review and audit supplier service delivery.

title: Managing changes to supplier services
control_objective: Changes to the provision of services by suppliers, including maintaining and improving existing information security policies, procedures and controls, should be managed, taking account of the criticality of business information, systems and processes involved and re­assessment of risks.

title: Responsibilities and procedures
control_objective: Management responsibilities and procedures should be established to ensure a quick, effective and orderly response to information security incidents.

title: Reporting information security events
control_objective: Information security events should be reported through appropriate management channels as quickly as possible.

title: Reporting information security weaknesses
control_objective: Employees and contractors using the organisation’s information systems and services should be required to note and report any observed or suspected information security weaknesses in systems or services.

title: Assessment of and decision on information security events
control_objective: Information security events should be assessed and it should be decided if they are to be classified as information security incidents.

title: Response to information security incidents
control_objective: Information security incidents should be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.

title: Learning from information security incidents
control_objective: Knowledge gained from analysing and resolving information security incidents should be used to reduce the likelihood or impact of future incidents.

title: Collection of evidence
control_objective: The organisation should define and apply procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition and preservation of information, which can serve as evidence.

title: Planning information security continuity
control_objective: The organization should determine its requirements for information security and the continuity of information security management in adverse situations, e.g. during a crisis or disaster.

title: Implementing information security continuity
control_objective: The organization should establish, document, implement and maintain processes, procedures and controls to ensure the required level of continuity for information security during an adverse situation.

title: Verify, review and evaluate information security continuity
control_objective:
The organization should verify the established and implemented information security continuity controls at regular intervals in order to ensure that they are valid and effective during adverse
situations.

title: Availability of information processing facilities
control_objective: Information processing facilities should be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.

title: Identification of applicable legislation and contractual requirements
control_objective: All relevant legislative statutory, regulatory, contractual requirements and the organisation’s approach to meet these requirements should be explicitly identified, documented and kept up to date for each information system and the organisation.

title: Intellectual property rights
control_objective: Appropriate procedures should be implemented to ensure compliance with legislative, regulatory and contractual requirements related to intellectual property rights and use of proprietary software products.

title: Protection of records
control_objective: Records should be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorised access and unauthorised release, in accordance with legislatory, regulatory, contractual and business requirements.

title: Privacy and protection of personally identifiable information
control_objective: Privacy and protection of personally identifiable information should be ensured as required in relevant legislation and regulation where applicable.

title: Regulation of cryptographic controls
control_objective: Cryptographic controls should be used in compliance with all relevant agreements, legislation and regulations.

title: Independent review of information security
control_objective: The organisation’s approach to managing information security and its implementation (i.e. control objectives, controls, policies, processes and procedures for information security) should be reviewed independently at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.

title: Compliance with security policies and standards
control_objective: Managers should regularly review the compliance of information processing and procedures within their area of responsibility with the appropriate security policies, standards and any other security requirements.

title: Technical compliance review
control_objective: Information systems should be regularly reviewed for compliance with the organisation’s information security policies and standards.
